translation.content: The nerdy sanctum of psychedelic techno is a notoriously hard world to break into: only the very best make the grade. Every now and then a DJ comes along who proves their mettle by making your head spin on its axis. Already part of the club firmament in her native Japan, DJ MARIA. broke through in Europe last year with sets at major spaces like Bassiani, FOLD and public records, flooring crowds with tapestries of acid, trance and techno that strike a perfect balance between vibrancy, impact and restraint. Born in Osaka and now based in Tokyo, DJ MARIA. joins a decorated lineage, cut from a similarly explorative cloth as Wata Igarashi, Haruka, ¥ØU$UK€ ¥UK1MAT$U and DJ Nobu. She discovered dance music as a 13-year-old, captivated by DVDs of legendary psytrance raves in Japan like Solstice and Vision Quest. 2004's Live From Tokyo, a filmed performance by Son Kite (AKA Swedish duo Minilogue), left an indelible mark. It wasn't until 2014 that she started DJing, balancing gigs with shifts at Tokyo's Face Records and, as of late, motherhood. Today, as well as touring and producing, she helps run two boutique forest festivals—Moment and Transcendence—both of which play a big part in upholding Japan's storied deep techno tradition. Though her catalogue as a recording artist is expanding, DJ MARIA. has reached these heights principally because of her exceptional talent as a DJ, which is on full display on this week's RA Podcast. The two-hour mix is pure manna for psychedelic techno heads, though we're confident the depth of feeling, subtle pacing and seamless stitching will lure in fans from across the electronic spectrum. Honestly, RA.971 is a total treasure—the latest wow moment from an artist destined to have a career littered with them. Press play and enter the wormhole. – Carlos Hawthorn What have you been up to recently? After successfully completing my gigs in 2024 and welcoming the New Year, I took the opportunity to refresh both my mind and body by visiting numerous hot springs. Honestly, hot springs are simply the best, haha. Since returning from my holiday, I've been working on music production every day and preparing for the outdoor parties that I organise. Whenever I need to recharge, I make it a habit to take walks to shrines and temples. How and where was the mix recorded? And can you tell us the idea behind it? I recorded this at home. About two months ago, my music management folder got completely disorganised. I'm not sure what caused it, but I spent several days sorting through all the tracks I had purchased over the years. That process turned out to be a great opportunity for self-reflection. It helped me gain a deeper understanding of what kind of sounds I love and the style of performance I want to create. I realised that I absolutely love psychedelic sounds, regardless of genre! For this mix, I incorporated many of my favourite sounds while focusing on showcasing their unique charm to the fullest. What are you looking forward to in 2025? I'm really looking forward to performing at festivals and clubs I've never visited before. In the past, I didn't have much time for sightseeing during tours, but this year, I want to make time and room to experience and enjoy the natural beauty of various places around the world. Moreover, I'm excited to take my track production to the next phase by working even harder and creating higher-quality sounds. If you had to choose one record from the past 25 years to save—either for DJing or that means the most to you—what would that be? Jake Stephenson - Selected Works: 1995-1999. I was really torn because there are so many things I love! But the record used for the second track in this mix, a reissue from Transmigration, is truly mystical and one of my absolute favourites. Correction, January 14th: A previous version of this article called ¥ØU$UK€ ¥UK1MAT$U a Future Terror resident. He isn't.
translation.blurb: Enter the wormhole with one of the techno artists we're most excited about in 2025.
